programme.desc: A panel of journalists provides reporting and analysis of the major stories emanating from the United States capital.

programme.title: Firing Line With Margaret Hoover
programme.desc: Rep. Jim Clyburn (D-S.C.) discusses race in American politics and his warning for younger generations.

programme.title: Martha Graham Dance Company: We Are Our Time
programme.desc: Following the Graham dancers from rehearsal to premiere as they carry Martha Graham's legacy forward with a new work.

programme.title: Great Performances
programme.desc: Following a spinal cord injury, Tiler Peck fights her way back as an elite dancer and choreographer with the New York City Ballet.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Animals travel in and out of cities for food, shelter and to start a family.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Stories of animals that survive despite being displaced by the growth of cities.

programme.title: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood
programme.desc: Fred discusses death when his fish dies.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: Adventures in Budylon; Ladonna Compson: Party Animal
programme.desc: D.W. and Bud take a wilderness adventure; Ladonna is determined to experience all the activities fall has to offer.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.sub-title: Back in Creature Time: Tasmanian Tiger
programme.desc: The Wild Kratts use Aviva's Time Trampoline again, this time to visit the extinct Tasmanian Tiger.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: A foggy Halloween almost derails Corky and Lily's trick-or-treating.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.desc: Lyla and a new friend build a spaceship; Lyla learns her sisters can predict what the other wants, so she tries to do the same with Luke.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.sub-title: The Magic Trick Collection; Listen Coach
programme.desc: Carl and Nico argue over who gets Arugula as their magician's assistant; Sheldon tries to help Carl become a good listener.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: Daniel's Important Job at the Market; Daniel's Important Job at the Enchanted Garden
programme.desc: Daniel and Dad help Prince Tuesday at the market; Daniel is excited to plant flowers but is upset when he has to do the weeding.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.sub-title: Birthday Dance Surprise; The Green Pepper
programme.desc: Phoebe plans a birthday surprise for Grandma; Phoebe and Jay must tend to the bell pepper in the garden.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: While Abby and Elmo work together to find a lost Bubble Bug, Grover and Cookie Monster stay behind to take care of the fairy garden's magical beasties.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.sub-title: The Friendship Day Dance; The Friendship Day Light Show
programme.desc: Mr. E learns a dance for Friendship Day; Super, the Wombats and the Boo Boo Blimp to the rescue when the power goes out for the Friendship Daylight Show.

programme.title: Donkey Hodie
programme.sub-title: Beach Day; Super Super Duper Sleepover
programme.desc: Duck enjoys "me time" at the beach; the pals like different things at Donkey's sleepover.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.sub-title: Rosie the Recycling Kid; Rosie's Mariposa
programme.desc: It's Recycling Day, so Rosie becomes a recycling kid, but she accidentally recycles Crystal's art project; when Rosie and Javi find a monarch butterfly in the backyard, they try to figure out what it needs so it can flutter again.

programme.title: Alma's Way
programme.sub-title: Rubberiest Rubber Bands; Donuts for Doggies
programme.desc: Alma and Rafia try to give away rubber bands; Alma and Rafia think they've invited too many people to Safina's bake sale.

programme.title: Cyberchase
programme.desc: A fiendish scheme causes all zeros to disappear, resulting in chaos.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: A made-to-order window trim factory; installing edge boards on a new deck; finishing the tongue-and-groove decking; landscaping plans.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: Building a backyard brick patio; new floors; weaving in salvaged floor strips with the original flooring.

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: Preventing sink drain clogs; how to test for lead-based paint; installing granite hillside steps.

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: How turfgrass seed is harvested and cleaned; breaking down popular paver types and walkway patterns; installing a porch swing.

programme.title: MotorWeek
programme.desc: Track testing the Porsche 911 GT3; Ford Maverick Lobo.

programme.title: A Chef's Life
programme.desc: Vivan prepares for her first trip to Feast Portland; old-school cabbage recipes.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tina Hobley and Sharon Maughan travel around the beautiful Cotswold countryside in search of some classy antiques to sell at auction. Accompanied by experts James Braxton and Raj Bisram, they face off for the bragging rights.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: Horizons From PBS News
programme.desc: Science, health, technology and environment experts and professionals address issues such as artificial intelligence, medicine and climate science.

programme.title: Firing Line With Margaret Hoover
programme.desc: Rep. Jim Clyburn (D-S.C.) discusses race in American politics and his warning for younger generations.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Cast iron lobster andirons circa 1880; a 1965 Roy Lichtenstein Shipboard Girl offset lithograph; a Japanese bronze lantern circa 1909.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Research into the ancestry of singer Lea Salonga and actress Amanda Seyfried reveals relatives who survived heart-wrenching ordeals.

programme.title: Breaking the Deadlock
programme.desc: Panelists explore the challenges and dilemmas around online sports gambling and its impact on young men.

programme.title: White With Fear
programme.desc: An explosive deep dive into the decades-long quest by America's conservative political machine to amass power by exploiting racial fault lines and stoking narratives of White victimization.

programme.title: Austin City Limits
programme.sub-title: Robert Glasper; DOMi and JD Beck
programme.desc: Grammy Award-winner Robert Glasper performs songs from "Black Radio"; DOMi and JD Beck perform tunes from their debut album "Not Tight."

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: The investigation into the March 2024 container ship collision that resulted in the collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore.

programme.title: Nature
programme.sub-title: Raptors: A Fistful of Daggers: Meet the Raptors
programme.desc: The many species of raptors, from giant eagles to miniature falconets, and how they survive bitter winters, learn to hunt and undergo epic migrations.

programme.title: Secrets of the Dead
programme.desc: Archaeological evidence suggests that the legend of King Arthur began in a 5th-century trading village following the departure of the Romans.

programme.title: Washington Week With The Atlantic
programme.desc: A panel of journalists provides reporting and analysis of the major stories emanating from the United States capital.

programme.title: Changing Planet
